ragina-intro.bodyText: RAGina is a mentalist RAG system. She reads HTML files and answers questions using TF-IDF retrieval and a free LLM. She works with Confluence exports, documentation folders, and any static HTML content.

confluence.bodyText: Confluence exports can be messy with nested folders and inconsistent HTML. RAGina handles this by scraping all HTML files recursively, extracting visible body text, and indexing it. She ignores scripts, styles, and navigation elements.

how-to-use.bodyText: To use RAGina, add a single script tag to your HTML page. Configure indexUrl to point to your JSON knowledge index, or use the folder picker to load HTML files directly. RAGina chunks text, builds TF-IDF index, and answers questions instantly.

powers.bodyText: RAGina uses TF-IDF vector scoring for semantic search, feeds relevant chunks to an LLM, and grounds answers in actual documents to prevent hallucinations. She finds connections others miss and delivers answers with sassy attitude.
